scope: Real submodule auto-fetch (ai_code_stack/installer.py ensure_submodules), new ai_code_stack/global_install.py module (writes generated config into ~/.codex, ~/.claude/agents, ~/.cursor/rules only for platforms detected as installed, never overwrites hand-written files), global-install CLI subcommand + scripts/global_install.py + scripts/global-install.ps1 + global-install.sh, macOS '.command' and Windows '.bat' double-click launchers, new tests (tests/test_global_install.py, additions to tests/test_installer_and_adapters.py), and README fixes to describe real installer/global-install behavior instead of the previously-unimplemented Node/Bun/Codex-doctoring and global-config-placement claims.
review_method: manual diff review + repo-wide secret-pattern grep over the full staged diff (api key, password, private key, AWS/GitHub/Slack token shapes) + subprocess/command-construction review of the two new subprocess call sites + review of the file-overwrite safety mechanism (atomic_write_text refuses to overwrite any target lacking the GENERATED BY AI CODE STACK marker) exercised by dedicated tests
notes: ensure_submodules() runs `git -C <root> submodule update --init -- vendors/<name>` with argv passed as a list (no shell=True); vendor names come from versions.lock (a repo-controlled trusted file), not from external/user input.

global_install.py's Codex MCP config writer builds a TOML fragment from the graphify-mcp binary path returned by shutil.which() (a trusted local filesystem value) and single-quote-escapes it before insertion; it merges into the existing config.toml via a scoped regex that only touches the [mcp_servers.graphify] block, verified by a test that asserts pre-existing config content survives untouched.

The new global write paths (~/.codex/AGENTS.md, ~/.claude/agents/*.md, ~/.cursor/rules/ai-code-stack.mdc) all go through the existing, already-tested atomic_write_text() safety net: a target that exists and lacks the GENERATED BY AI CODE STACK marker raises FileExistsError and is reported as skipped, never overwritten. This is exercised directly by test_apply_never_overwrites_hand_written_file, which was run against real pre-existing hand-written agent files on this machine (~/.claude/agents/security-reviewer.md, architect.md, planner.md) in manual verification, confirming they are left untouched.

Antigravity is unconditionally skipped for global placement (no verified global config file format exists for it), matching the codebase's existing rule against fabricating unverified platform capabilities.
No authentication, authorization, payment, session, or user-data code paths were touched. Secret-pattern scan over the full staged diff returned zero matches.
